title: Takthok Monastery
tagline: Takthok Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Takthok Monastery is a much-loved spiritual town in Ladakh (UT), known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: Takthok Monastery (tib. བྲག་ཐོག་ Wylie = Brag Thog, pron. Trag Thog, "Stone Roof" in English) is a Buddhist monastery in Sakti village in Ladakh, northern India, located approximately 46 kilometres east of Leh. It is the only monastery in Ladakh belonging to the Nying-ma-pa or Red Hat sect.

topPlaces.name: Sakti, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Sakti (Tibetan: གསེར་ཁྲི་, Wylie: sag kri) is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Rambir Por
topPlaces.description: Rambir Por is a village in Leh district of Ladakh in India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Chemrey
topPlaces.description: Chemrey (also known as Chemdey) is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Kharu tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Chemrey Monastery
topPlaces.description: Chemrey Monastery or Chemrey Gompa in Indus River valley is a 1664 Buddhist monastery in Leh district of Ladakh in northern India. It is 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) north of Hemis Monastery, 46 kilometres (29 mi) southeast of Leh and 24 kilometres (15 mi) northwest of Upshi.

topPlaces.name: Karu, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Kharu (Wylie: mkhar bu, THL: khar bu) also spelt Karu, is a town, subdivision, tehsil, block, tourist station and military base on the bank of Indus River in the Leh district of Union territory of Ladakh in India. It is located 34 km southeast of Leh on Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.

topPlaces.name: Nang, Leh
topPlaces.description: Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

Chemrey Monastery or Chemrey Gompa in Indus River valley is a 1664 Buddhist monastery in Leh district of Ladakh in northern India. It is 6 kilometres (3.7 mi) north of Hemis Monastery, 46 kilometres (29 mi) southeast of Leh and 24 kilometres (15 mi) northwest of Upshi.

Kharu (Wylie: mkhar bu, THL: khar bu) also spelt Karu, is a town, subdivision, tehsil, block, tourist station and military base on the bank of Indus River in the Leh district of Union territory of Ladakh in India. It is located 34 km southeast of Leh on Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.
Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Takthok Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Takthok Monastery is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Takthok Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (289 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Takthok Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Takthok Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Takthok Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Takthok Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Takthok Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sakti, Ladakh, Rambir Por, Chemrey, Chemrey Monastery, Karu, Ladakh.
